title: Session responsibility for budgeting and the diaconate's proper role
sub: The CCB responded that BCO 10-2 already assigns budgeting to the session, so no special instruction to sessions was necessary.

title: Session oversight of a church lacking a full session
sub: The CCB directed Rev. Dunkerley to BCO 12-2, noting that church courts have latitude to establish rules not violating the Constitution when a congregation has only one ruling elder and no full session.

title: Meaning and manner of reaffirmation of faith for membership
sub: The CCB advised that 'reaffirmation of faith' historically means answering BCO 58-5 questions 1 and 2, but a testimony of Christian experience may also satisfy it, and requesting reaffirmation of transferring members is not improper.

title: Ordination questions and re-ordination for ministers from other denominations
sub: The CCB advised that BCO 13-6 requires ordination questions be put to any minister from another denomination, and presbytery may require re-ordination if deemed necessary.

title: Ordination questions and re-ordination for ministers from other denominations
sub: The CCB advised that BCO 13-6 requires ordination questions be put to any minister from another denomination, and presbytery may require re-ordination if deemed necessary.

title: Applicability of new internship and licensure requirement to candidates already in process
sub: The GA adopted a procedure specifying that the new required year of licensure applies only to candidates not yet in the ordination process at the time of adoption.
disposition: adopted — internship requirement applies only to new candidates not yet in process

title: General Assembly committee authority over cooperative mission agreements
sub: The CCB advised that the Committee of Commissioners channels business to the floor rather than acting as a commission, and the full Assembly retains authority over cooperative agreements.

title: Ordination status and restoration process after deposition from office
sub: The CCB advised that a deposed officer has no ordination status, and restoration requires at minimum reaffirmation of vows, laying on of hands, and a proper call.

title: Definition of 'voter' in congregational officer elections
sub: A voter under BCO 24-3 is a communing member in good standing present at the meeting; only those who cast a ballot count toward the majority required for election.

title: Constitutionality of a non-geographical presbytery under BCO 11-4
sub: The CCB found that the BCO does not address non-geographical presbyteries, as BCO 11-4 implies geographical bounds while BCO 13-1 does not limit 'bounds' geographically.

title: Presbytery authority to judge theonomy as heresy under WCF 19-4
sub: The CCB advised that a presbytery retains the right to examine and judge a man heretical on WCF 19-4 grounds, provided the error is demonstrated by specific evidence.

title: Scope of congregational meetings beyond BCO-enumerated purposes
sub: The CCB advised that BCO 25-2 permits congregational meetings to be called for anything deemed in the best interests of the church, beyond the stated examples.

title: Presbytery approval required for amendments to pastoral call terms
sub: Because BCO 20-1 requires presbytery approval of the original call, the CCB advised that any subsequent changes to the terms of a pastor's call also require presbytery approval.

title: Prohibition of dual candidacy under BCO 18-7
sub: BCO 18-7 permits a candidate to retain home-church membership while under another presbytery's care, but does not allow simultaneous candidacy under two presbyteries.

title: Whether a ruling elder moderator must also hold a representative commission
sub: The CCB advised that a non-representative ruling elder may serve as presbytery moderator, but may only vote when his vote would be determinative.

title: Power of a receiving court to broaden charges in a transferred judicial case
sub: When a judicial case is transferred, the receiving court becomes court of original jurisdiction and may add further charges pertaining to the original matter, but wholly new charges must go back to the first court.

title: Teaching elder without a call and BCO 13-5 exceptions
sub: The CCB advised that BCO 13-5 allows a presbytery to retain a teaching elder without a call in cases it deems necessary, subject to General Assembly review.

title: Scope and duration of BCO debate prohibitions during a complaint
sub: BCO prohibitions on debate apply only to the specific matter complained, only until final adjudication, and to all members of the lower court — expiring upon final resolution.

title: Recourse when General Assembly may have erred in a decision
sub: The CCB advised that errors alleged during a GA meeting may be challenged under BCO 45, and subsequent assemblies may take contrary positions but cannot annul prior actions.

title: Whether congregation may set minimum voting age for elections
sub: The BCO provides no minimum voting age for congregational meetings, except where state law imposes one for corporate votes.

title: Minimum ruling elders required to constitute a valid session
sub: The CCB advised that one ruling elder plus a pastor constitutes a valid session, but a church without a pastor requires at least two ruling elders.

title: Ordination of men who believe women may serve as deacons
sub: Believing Scripture permits women deacons, while agreeing to abide by BCO standards in practice, is not by itself sufficient grounds to deny ordination or reception in the PCA.

title: Propriety of abstractly listing disqualifying beliefs for ordination
sub: The GA declined to determine abstractly which beliefs disqualify a man from office, holding that only proper judicial processes may make such binding determinations.

title: Handling exceptions at ordination and distinctions between BCO and confessional exceptions
sub: Courts must investigate stated exceptions; BCO exceptions under vow 3 are handled differently from doctrinal exceptions under vow 2, with judicial process available if warranted.

title: Eligibility of expired-term trustee to fill another trustee's unexpired term
sub: The CCB advised that BCO 14-1-12 permits such a nomination only if the agency's assembly-approved bylaws explicitly authorize it.

title: Trustee with expired term filling another's unexpired vacancy
sub: The CCB reaffirmed that BCO 14-1-12 permits an expired-term trustee to fill an unexpired vacancy only if agency bylaws approved by the Assembly specifically allow it.

title: Administrative Committee acting as an unlimited commission under Standing Rule 16
sub: The CCB advised that Standing Rule 16 effectively created an unlimited commission contrary to BCO 15-1, which restricts commissions to specifically defined matters.

title: Proper limits on presbyterial use of the extraordinary clause
sub: The GA counseled that use of BCO 21-4's extraordinary clause must be limited to truly extraordinary circumstances or proven extraordinary gifts, warning against increasing laxity in application.

title: Presbytery authority to conduct regular visitation of congregations
sub: The CCB advised that BCO 13-9 grants presbytery the prerogative to initiate regular visitation of its churches for their spiritual welfare.

title: Meaning of willful neglect and gross crime under BCO 46-5 and 30-4
sub: BCO 46-5 requires due process under BCO 27-5 for incorrigible members, and 'gross crime or heresy' is to be understood in its biblical and moral sense as determined by the church court.

title: Proper limits on presbyterial use of the extraordinary clause
sub: The GA counseled that use of BCO 21-4's extraordinary clause must be limited to truly extraordinary circumstances or proven extraordinary gifts, warning against increasing laxity in application.

title: Voting rights of an associate pastor when not moderating the session
sub: The CCB advised that an associate pastor, as a session member, retains full voting rights even when not serving as moderator.

title: Voting rights of Elders and Deacons Emeriti after BCO 24-9 amendment
sub: The CCB noted that the Twelfth GA amended BCO 24-9 to prohibit Emeriti from voting, while a grandfather clause preserved prior voting privileges for those already holding them.

title: Notice of complaint sufficiency and timing of suspension motion under BCO 43-4
sub: Northeast Presbytery asked whether a written statement of intent to complain constitutes adequate notice to suspend a court's action and when a suspension motion is procedurally in order.

title: Scope of presbytery commissions under BCO 15-2
sub: The CCB advised that BCO 15-2's list of matters assignable to commissions is typical but not exhaustive, with other matters assignable subject to review and control.

title: Applicability of extraordinary clause to ministers from other denominations
sub: The CCB advised that neither BCO 13-6 nor BCO 21-4 contains an extraordinary clause applicable to ministers transferring from other denominations.

title: Definition of 'appearance' before an adjudicating commission under BCO 42-11
sub: The CCB defined 'appearance' as the personal presence of a party or counsel before the adjudicating body after that body has given reasonable notice.

title: Constituting a judicial commission under BCO 15-2
sub: A judicial commission requires both appointment under BCO 15-2 with instructions to adjudicate and prior approval of charges for process under BCO 32-3.

title: Teaching elder voting rights and congregational approval of pastoral salary changes
sub: The CCB advised that a teaching elder votes as a full session member, and that any change to pastoral salary terms requires congregational approval as part of the call.

title: Admitting non-members in good standing to the Lord's Table
sub: The GA left to the spiritual discernment of the session whether to permit a person who is not a member in good standing of an evangelical church to participate in the Lord's Supper.
disposition: left to spiritual discernment of Session

title: Liberty granted by exceptions versus right to teach contrary to standards
sub: Ordination with exceptions grants personal liberty to hold minority views but does not warrant public teaching or preaching of those views in ways that disturb the church's peace and purity.

title: Legality of dividing presbytery into sections to hear candidate sermons
sub: The full presbytery must conduct all trial parts; dividing into sections to hear candidate sermons for ordination is impermissible except as a commission and should be treated as exceptional.

title: Teaching elder voting rights and congregational approval of pastoral salary changes
sub: The CCB advised that a teaching elder votes as a full session member, and that any change to pastoral salary terms requires congregational approval as part of the call.

title: Grounds and procedures for removing members from PCA church rolls
sub: Declining to address case-specific questions, the CCB outlined the three BCO provisions by which members may be removed from church rolls: transfer, discipline, or relocation.
disposition: declined to answer specific questions; general advice given

title: Whether deletion from the roll under BCO 46-5 constitutes excommunication
sub: The CCB answered yes — 'deletion' under BCO 46-5 is excommunication in effect, and the due-process procedures of BCO 27-5 must therefore be followed.

title: Procedure for removing members who relocate without affiliating elsewhere
sub: BCO 46-2 governs removal of members who move away and fail to affiliate within a year; BCO 46-5's formal discipline applies only to willful neglect, not mere relocation.

title: Teaching elder voting rights and congregational approval of pastoral salary changes
sub: The CCB advised that a teaching elder votes as a full session member, and that any change to pastoral salary terms requires congregational approval as part of the call.

title: Rotating sessions, ballot control, and officer election procedures
sub: The CCB answered five questions on officer elections: the session must present all eligible men, the congregation determines the number elected, and no constitutional limit restricts election frequency beyond a 30-day notice.

title: Effect of GA refusal to accept a judicial reference on lower-court proceedings
sub: The committee's recommendation and the Assembly's determination refusing a judicial reference apply to the entire judicial process in PCA courts, clarifying that no parties were left without recourse.

title: Written sermon requirement for licensure under BCO 19-2
sub: Both a written and oral sermon are required for licensure; the written sermon must be available to the full presbytery, while the oral sermon may be presented before the presbytery or its committee.

title: Effect of appeal on suspension of disciplinary censure during review
sub: The CCB advised that a judgment of divestiture is suspended during appeal to the General Assembly unless the lower court separately places the censure into immediate effect.

title: Call of assistant pastor from another denomination without presbyterial examination
sub: A session may not call an ordained assistant pastor from another denomination without presbytery approval and examination, as all teaching elders must be members of the serving presbytery.

title: Judicial power of visitation commissions absent formal charges
sub: A presbytery commission visiting a church affected with disorder may not be granted judicial power when no charges exist and no case is pending.

title: Whether congregation may dissolve the session by vote
sub: A congregation cannot dissolve the session; it may by majority vote request individual officer removals under BCO 24-6, but the final decision rests with the session, and presbytery may assume jurisdiction under BCO 13-9.

title: Mandatory review of all previously unreviewed session minutes
sub: The CCB advised that it is mandatory for a presbytery to review all previously unreviewed session records, not merely resume with current ones.

title: Whether deletion from the roll under BCO 46-5 constitutes excommunication
sub: The CCB answered yes — 'deletion' under BCO 46-5 is excommunication in effect, and the due-process procedures of BCO 27-5 must therefore be followed.

title: Legality of dividing presbytery into sections to hear candidate sermons
sub: The full presbytery must conduct all trial parts; dividing into sections to hear candidate sermons for ordination is impermissible except as a commission and should be treated as exceptional.

title: Informal versus formal admonition and due-process requirements under BCO 27-5
sub: The CCB distinguished informal admonition (ordinary relational correction) from formal judicial admonition, holding that the paragraph in question did not require BCO 27-5 due process.

title: Erasure of a teaching elder seeking transfer to independent church
sub: A presbytery wishing to release a minister to an independent church should handle it by erasure under BCO 38-3, which does not affect his ordination unless the church is heretical.

title: Deacons delegating the treasurer role to a non-deacon congregation member
sub: The CCB advised that delegating the treasurer's responsibilities to a non-deacon member of the congregation does not conflict with BCO 9-4.

title: Emeritus officers voting in higher courts and local bodies
sub: The CCB affirmed that an Elder Emeritus may not vote in the local session but may vote as a delegate to Presbytery if appointed to serve there.

title: Stated supply's role as session moderator and congregational meeting chair
sub: The CCB advised that a stated supply becomes moderator only by session invitation or presbytery appointment, and a session member, not the stated supply, should open congregational meetings.

title: Role of a presbytery evangelist in church discipline and membership actions
sub: The CCB advised that an evangelist may grant membership transfers but may not remove names from rolls or impose discipline, as those are acts reserved to church courts.

title: Jurisdiction over candidates and licentiates moving to a new field
sub: The BCO does not specify which presbytery holds jurisdiction over candidates or licentiates moving to a new field, leaving each case to the receiving presbytery's discretion.

title: Dividing presbytery into parts to hear ordination candidate sermons
sub: BCO 21-4 does not permit presbyteries to divide simply into committees to hear ordination sermons; division into commissions is not prohibited but should be exceptional, not regular practice.

title: Scope of the extraordinary clause for ordination candidates
sub: The CCB advised that under BCO 18-2 the extraordinary clause excuses only the six-month membership period, not the session endorsement, the two-month filing deadline, or the one-year internship sequence.

title: Extraordinary clause exemptions from licensure examinations
sub: In extraordinary cases a presbytery may exempt a candidate from any of the prescribed parts of the licensure examination under BCO 19-2, though the clause must not be used as a subterfuge to avoid historic training requirements.

title: Pre-care service counting toward internship year under BCO 19-7
sub: A presbytery may designate service rendered before a candidate came under care or was licensed—such as service as a ruling elder supply—as fulfilling the internship requirement under BCO 19-16.

title: Scope of extraordinary clause relative to educational prerequisites
sub: BCO 21-4's extraordinary clause applies only to listed trial parts for ordination, not to prerequisite requirements such as college diploma, seminary diploma, and internship completion.

title: Session limiting eligible nominees on officer election ballot
sub: A session may not withhold qualified nominees from the congregation's ballot to limit board size; BCO 24-1 requires all eligible men to be reported to the congregation.

title: Dissolution of pastoral charge outside presbytery's jurisdiction
sub: BCO 23-1 does not apply to non-PCA churches outside presbytery jurisdiction; a congregational vote cannot substitute for that chapter's dissolution process.

title: Membership status and session authority upon dissolution of a congregation
sub: Upon dissolution of a congregation, the session terminates, membership in the visible church is dissolved for those not transferred, and Presbytery may not unilaterally assign members to other churches.

title: Whether a resignation letter constitutes willful neglect justifying roll erasure
sub: The CCB held that 'deletion' under BCO 46-5 is equivalent to excommunication and referred the question of easier non-judicial removal to the 17th GA for potential BCO amendment.

title: Session authority over officer nominees and inactive elders under rotation
sub: A session must present all qualified nominees for election and may not use BCO 24-6 to exclude inactive officers; presbytery proceedings are not automatically required for long-inactive elders.

title: Certification of presbytery dismissal by committee chairman's letter
sub: A letter from a presbytery committee chairman is insufficient evidence of dismissal; BCO 46-6 requires a certificate bearing the stated clerk's signature per BCO 10-4.

title: Applicability of BCO 10-3 sermon and moderator-absence procedures to presbyteries
sub: The CCB advised that BCO 10-3 is ambiguous on moderator absence, leaving each presbytery to interpret it, and ruled that telephone conference meetings are impermissible.

title: Implementation of 1988 BCO amendments on Assistant Pastors
sub: Tennessee Valley Presbytery asked whether the new BCO 22-3/22-4 Assistant Pastor provisions applied retroactively, to all full-time ministers, and whether a lesser examination was appropriate.

title: Presbytery standing rule exempting retired ministers from reporting
sub: Illiana Presbytery's proposed rule relieving retired ministers from mandatory reporting was found not in conflict with BCO 23-2, with the presbytery advised to use its own wisdom under BCO 41-5.

title: Re-examination and divestiture of officers in rotational systems
sub: The CCB advised that the BCO provides no procedure for re-examining rotated-off officers' qualifications, but divestiture may only occur under BCO 24-6, 38-2, or 34-10.

title: Congregational and presbyterial approval of call term changes
sub: Changes to a pastoral call's terms require both congregational approval under BCO 20-6 and presbytery approval, consistent with the 11th General Assembly's prior ruling on BCO 20-1.

title: Presbytery jurisdiction and restoration path for a deposed and repentant minister
sub: The CCB advised that restoration of a deposed minister follows BCO 37-4 and 37-7 exclusively, and BCO 18–21 ordination procedures do not apply.

title: Intern preaching without licensure and definition of regular basis
sub: An intern may complete his internship and test his preaching gift without being licensed, and each presbytery may determine what constitutes preaching on a regular basis.

title: Proper use of memorials and finality of adjudicated GA decisions
sub: A memorial under BCO 40-5 is the proper recourse for alleging GA error after adjournment; the Assembly will not reopen a finally adjudicated case except to correct a manifest error.

title: Meaning of 'stay of proceedings' under BCO 40-5
sub: Although 'stay of further proceedings' technically means holding a matter in abeyance, the CCB found that the 17th and 18th General Assemblies clearly intended the stay to finally terminate the matter.

title: Voluntary resignation from membership and application of BCO 46-5
sub: The GA clarified that BCO 46-5 had been variously interpreted regarding voluntary resignation, resolving that a simple resignation does not automatically trigger the willful-neglect excommunication process.

title: Voluntary association principle applied to individual church membership
sub: The CCB affirmed that the principle of voluntary association governing churches also applies to individuals joining a local PCA congregation.

title: Minister from another denomination serving as stated supply
sub: A minister from another denomination licensed by a PCA presbytery may serve as stated supply but may not administer sacraments unless his denomination has fraternal relations with the PCA or his sacramental views are consistent with PCA standards.

title: SJC authority to issue decisions contradicting prior General Assembly actions
sub: The CCB advised that the SJC may adjudicate cases differently from prior Assembly actions, and that SJC decisions are not debatable on the Assembly floor.

title: Review and control action while a judicial case is pending on appeal
sub: A higher court may note an exception through review and control but may take no action on it while judicial proceedings are pending; the effect of any unconstitutional act is suspended upon appeal.

title: Right to complain against a court's reception of an intemperate protest
sub: A presbyter may complain against a court's decision to receive a protest alleged to contain intemperate or disrespectful language, since that reception is itself a court action under BCO 43-1.

title: Permissible scope of communion practice under BCO 58-4
sub: The GA denied closed communion (members of the local congregation only) as incompatible with BCO 58-4, affirming open or close communion while requiring baptism as a prerequisite to the Table.

title: Sequence of placing a call versus examining a candidate under BCO 21-1
sub: The CCB found ambiguity in BCO 21-1 regarding whether a call must be placed before or after examination, and advised that existing presbytery practice of each presbytery determining its own sequence should prevail until the GA acts.
disposition: advice given — ambiguity exists; current practice (each presbytery determines sequence) should prevail

title: Constitutionality of GA directive to omit financial amounts from presbytery minutes
sub: The CCB advised that directing presbyteries to omit financial amounts from call-change records was constitutional as an exercise of GA oversight, but any actual editing of an already-adopted minute record was unconstitutional.
disposition: advice given — action partially constitutional, editing of record unconstitutional

title: Referent of 'it' in BCO 15-1 regarding commission records
sub: The CCB concluded that 'it' in BCO 15-1 line 6 refers to the concluding actions of the commission, not the full record of proceedings, consistent with BCO 15-3's requirement that the commission's judgment be entered as the presbytery's action.
disposition: advice given — 'it' refers to concluding actions of the commission

title: Parliamentary procedure for adopting committee reports at General Assembly
sub: RAO 17-3 requires separate adoption of each committee resolution, superseding Robert's Rules' normal practice of a single motion to adopt the full report.

title: Congregational vote required on pastor's financial severance
sub: BCO 23-1 requires a congregational vote on a financial severance package when a pastor resigns his call without another call.
disposition: advice given — yes, congregation must vote on financial severance

title: Judicial process against a member who resigns from the roll
sub: The CCB declined to advise, finding the reference improper, but noted that GA Judicial Case 93-3 does not bar full formal discipline of a member who tenders a letter of resignation.
disposition: declined to advise; reference returned as improper

title: Ordination of candidate with exceptions to six-day creation and male-only office
sub: New River Presbytery's response to prior exceptions was found satisfactory by the GA, though the Review Committee urged the presbytery not to approve future candidates whose exceptions deny fundamentals such as six-day creation or male-only ordination.

title: Meaning of 'emergency' and validity of supermajority threshold for called presbytery meeting
sub: The CCB advised that 'emergency' carries its plain dictionary meaning and that requiring concurrence of six of eight officers before calling a special meeting violates BCO 13-12 by overriding the rights of the smaller group entitled to call such meetings.
disposition: advice given — procedure in conflict with BCO 13-12

title: Re-examination of previously ordained officer nominees by session
sub: The CCB advised that all nominees for ruling elder or deacon, including previously ordained individuals, must be re-examined by the session, which retains the right to find them deficient.
disposition: advice given — re-examination required; session may find nominee deficient

title: Six-month PCA membership requirement for ministerial candidacy from non-PCA church
sub: The CCB advised that BCO 18-2 probably requires PCA congregation membership for candidacy, but acknowledged varying presbytery practice and recommended an overture to clarify whether endorsement from a non-PCA ruling body is permissible.
disposition: advice given — BCO 18-2 probably requires PCA membership; overture recommended

title: Proposed BCO amendment permitting minimum voting age for members
sub: A proposed amendment to BCO 11-2 that would have set a minimum voting age for communicant members was defeated by the presbyteries.

title: Declaring exceptions for non-literal six-day creationist views
sub: The CCB declined to act, ruling that defining what constitutes an exception to the standards is beyond its scope; presbyteries wishing to set such a norm should overture the GA.
disposition: declined to take action — reference not proper; overture recommended for proper procedure

title: Perfecting amendment language for removing members from the roll
sub: The CCB drafted amended BCO 38-3 language governing roll removal when a member joins another church, with options for retaining or removing names when charges are pending.
disposition: advice given — proposed amended language for BCO 38-3 forwarded

title: Whether a church court may accept a member's resignation
sub: The CCB advised there is no constitutional bar preventing a court from accepting a member's resignation and removing that person's name from the roll.
disposition: advice given — no constitutional bar to accepting resignation

title: SJC authority to adjudicate issues not referred to it
sub: The CCB opined that the Standing Judicial Commission may not adjudicate any issue or matter not referred to it, as initiating new issues would improperly make the SJC a court of original jurisdiction.
disposition: advice given — SJC cannot adjudicate issues not referred to it

title: Setting a nomination deadline before the one-month officer election notice
sub: A session may set a nomination deadline before the one-month congregational meeting notice expires, but all six BCO 24-1 requirements for officer election must be met.
disposition: advice given — session may set nomination deadline before one-month meeting notice; all six required elements must be included

title: Whether presbytery may investigate before proceeding to trial
sub: BCO 31-2 permits a presbytery to investigate and determine strong presumption of guilt before adjudication; the CCB recommended amendments to BCO 31, 32, and 34 to clarify.
disposition: advice given — BCO 31-2 permits investigation before adjudication; amendments recommended

title: Dissolution of pastoral call and supermajority vote requirements
sub: BCO 23-1 refers back to BCO 20 for meeting procedure for pastoral dissolution; a 2/3 vote requirement must be explicitly adopted by the congregation to apply to dissolution.
disposition: advice given — BCO 23-1 refers back to BCO 20 for meeting and conduct; 2/3 vote for dissolution requires explicit congregational adoption

title: Alternate commission member serving as chairman or voting when not needed for quorum
sub: The CCB found no constitutional prohibition on an alternate member serving as commission chairman, and ruled that an alternate filling a principal's vacancy may vote even when not needed to constitute a quorum.
disposition: advice given — no constitutional prohibition on alternate serving as chairman or voting

title: Deacon and session authority to sell donated church property
sub: CCB advised that deacons, subject to session approval and congregational consent, hold authority to sell church property such as donated stock.

title: Receiving a missionary candidate unable to appear before presbytery in person
sub: The CCB advised that a presbytery may receive a missionary under care via commission or interactive electronic means to satisfy BCO 18-3's appearance requirement.

title: Whether SJC member vows conflict with BCO and Westminster Standards
sub: The CCB found SJC vows 4 and 5 'flawed' for failing to reference Scripture directly, and advised amending Vow 4 to resolve the ambiguity with BCO 39-3 and the WCF.

title: Use of 'appeal' versus 'complaint' in cases without process
sub: An overture's final sentence conflicted with the Constitution because appeals lie only in judicial cases; the CCB advised substituting 'complaint' for 'appeal' to cure the conflict.

title: Additional criteria for removing indefinite suspension from office or sacraments
sub: A proposed overture adding two criteria beyond BCO 37-3 for removing indefinite suspension was in conflict with the Constitution.

title: Establishing a minimum voting age for congregational meetings
sub: The CCB found an overture permitting a minimum voting age in congregational meetings in conflict with the Constitution, particularly BCO 6-4.

title: Whether a BCO 34-1 Case May Be Declared Without Being Heard
sub: The CCB advised Ascension Presbytery by referring to its contemporaneous answer to Western Carolina Presbytery that a case under BCO 34-1 may be declared without being heard.

title: Whether a BCO 34-1 Case May Be Declared Without Being Heard
sub: The CCB advised Ascension Presbytery by referring to its contemporaneous answer to Western Carolina Presbytery that a case under BCO 34-1 may be declared without being heard.

title: Which GA entities are subject to the permanent committee ineligibility rule
sub: The CCB clarified that the "permanent committee" ineligibility provision in RAO 13-2 applies only to the four committees enumerated in RAO 4-2, not to other GA entities.

title: Capitalization of 'Church' in BCO 57-5 membership vows
sub: The CCB advised James River Presbytery that the original text of BCO 57-5 uses an uppercase 'C' in 'Church' in both membership vows 4 and 5.

title: Re-ordination of former PCA ministers who were divested without censure
sub: The CCB found no technical constitutional conflict but raised concerns about the nature of ordination and inconsistency between restoration after deposition versus divestiture.

title: Disciplinary measures against a presbytery tried on GA floor
sub: An overture targeting Tennessee Valley Presbytery conflicted with the Constitution by predetermining guilt and prescribing censures without following BCO 40-4 through 40-6 or referral to the SJC.

title: Constitutional status of the Directory for Worship
sub: The CCB issued a majority rationale and minority report providing historical background and differing positions on the constitutional standing of BCO chapters 47–63.

title: Presbytery-wide withdrawal from the General Assembly
sub: The Constitution is silent on whether a whole presbytery may withdraw from the GA, though individual congregations may withdraw as a group with each congregation's consent.

title: Session authority to raise supermajority voting threshold for officers
sub: A Session cannot raise the voting requirement beyond a majority for calling a pastor, dissolving a pastoral relation, or electing a church officer.

title: Whether Presbytery Must Divest a Teaching Elder Without Call After Three Years
sub: The CCB advised that a presbytery is not required to divest a teaching elder without call after three years, but must inquire and exercise discretion after completing its inquiry.

title: Whether a session may set a minimum age for communicant membership
sub: The CCB advised that sessions must evaluate each membership request individually regardless of age, since the timing of credible faith cannot be precisely fixed.

title: No exceptions permitted in Lord's Supper administration practice
sub: The CCB advised Great Lakes Presbytery that BCO 58-4 allows no exception of practice in administering the Lord's Supper, and presbyteries should use BCO 34-5 when considering exceptions from candidates.

title: When a member may lodge a complaint with a higher court
sub: The CCB advised that a complainant may not go to a higher court until the lower court has acted on the complaint pursuant to BCO 43-2 and 43-3.

title: CCB Authority to Give Unsolicited Advice to Other Committees
sub: The CCB advised New River Presbytery that it lacks authority to give unsolicited advice to other committees, though the CRPR may make any recommendation within its RAO 16 purview to the GA.

title: Including the pastor on a search committee for an associate pastor
sub: The CCB found the overture in conflict with the constitution, as teaching elders are not congregational members and may not serve on the congregation's pulpit committee.

title: General Assembly authority to amend or delete an SJC concurring opinion
sub: The CCB advised that the GA may not amend the SJC's report by deleting a concurring opinion, since under BCO 15-5 an SJC decision is the final decision of the Assembly.

title: SJC authority to opine on legitimacy of General Assembly action
sub: The CCB advised that the SJC may only address matters assigned to it, but once assigned a matter may render an opinion on the legitimacy of the GA action that prompted the referral.

title: Temperateness review and status of SJC concurring and dissenting opinions
sub: The CCB held that determination of temperateness must occur before a concurring opinion is appended, and that such opinions represent only individual views, not the mind of the Church or binding SJC decisions.

title: Ordination vows require compliance with Assembly rules of order
sub: The CCB advised that delegates are bound by ordination vows to follow PCA constitutional rules, and that changing unscriptural standards requires proper amendment procedures, not suspension.

title: Proposed overture language for handling exceptions to Westminster Standards
sub: The CCB found the proposed overture in conflict with BCO Preliminary Principles, eroding presbyteries' exclusive authority to evaluate candidates' subscription.

title: Overture to abolish the Standing Judicial Commission
sub: The CCB found the overture to abolish the SJC in conflict with BCO 14-1 and 15, and also unconstitutionally vague about the basis for alternative decisions.

title: Proposed Procedure for Resolving Alleged Conflicts Between Scripture and the Constitution
sub: The CCB found Eastern Canada Presbytery's overture in conflict with the Constitution because it would allow a General Assembly to bypass constitutional processes and permit modification of commission reports.

title: Mandatory reporting of ordination exceptions to General Assembly
sub: An overture requiring presbyteries to record and report all ordination exceptions to the GA was found in conflict with the Constitution because it would allow a presbytery to bind a man's conscience beyond what the BCO permits.

title: Constitutional concerns with SJC procedures for hearing memorials
sub: The CCB identified significant constitutional issues with the SJC's memorial procedures, including an undefined term, due-process deficiencies, and potential conflict with historical Presbyterian usage.

title: Presbytery's right to reclaim a matter from a commission
sub: A court may reclaim a matter from a commission before completion, but may not reverse a lower court's decision without actually hearing the appeal per BCO 42.

title: General Assembly authority to mandate health coverage for all active ministers
sub: The CCB ruled that neither Scripture nor the constitution grants the General Assembly authority to mandate health insurance purchases, leading the Assembly to substitute a recommendation for a mandate.
disposition: advice given — not constitutional to mandate

title: Session's right to access congregation's financial and attendance records
sub: CCB advised that the session and diaconate have a right to access financial and attendance records as needed, limited only by prudence and protection of reputation.

title: Status of a presbytery case after SJC rules it out of order
sub: When the SJC rules a case judicially out of order, the case reverts substantively to the status it held when the presbytery completed its action.

title: Whether presbyteries must record ministers' confessional exceptions in minutes
sub: The CCB answered no: neither the PCA constitution nor the RAO then required a presbytery to record a minister's exceptions to the Westminster Standards or BCO in its minutes.

title: Presbytery authority to record candidates' disagreements with the Constitution
sub: The CCB found no conflict, though a minority held that requiring presbyteries to record doctrinal disagreements would erode their exclusive authority to determine subscription under BCO 21-5.

title: Specifying procedure for handling ordination exceptions in BCO 21-4
sub: The CCB majority found an overture specifying exception-handling procedures in conflict with the second ordination vow, though a minority disagreed and found no conflict.
disposition: in conflict (majority); minority reported not in conflict

title: Oversight of temperate language in SJC dissenting opinions
sub: The CCB found the overture in conflict with the constitution, as no mechanism exists for commissioners to object to language in a concluded judicial case.

title: Applying pastoral-election large-minority rule to ruling elders and deacons
sub: Applying BCO 20-5's large-minority provision to the election of ruling elders and deacons conflicts with BCO 24-1 and 24-3.

title: Presbytery authority to record candidates' disagreements with the Constitution
sub: The CCB found no conflict, though a minority held that requiring presbyteries to record doctrinal disagreements would erode their exclusive authority to determine subscription under BCO 21-5.

title: GA Stated Clerk reporting exceptions taken in presbyteries on ordination
sub: The overture was mostly not in conflict with the Constitution, but a final sentence risked eroding presbyteries' authority to determine confessional subscription.

title: Restricting presbytery and General Assembly voting to pastors only
sub: The CCB found the overture in conflict with BCO 3-1, which vests church power in the whole body, and BCO 14-2, which entitles all TEs to representation.

title: Overtures Committee authority to offer germane amendments to overtures
sub: The CCB found that permitting the Bills and Overtures Committee to amend overtures conflicted with the constitution by undermining presbyteries' right to propose measures to the Assembly.

title: Interpretation of 'papists' in WCF 24.3 for marriage eligibility
sub: The CCB declined Iowa Presbytery's reference asking whether 'papists' in WCF 24.3 means all Roman Catholics or only those holding Roman Catholic soteriology, finding no matter pending before the lower court.

title: Presbytery authority to remove censure of suspended or deposed teaching elder
sub: A new presbytery may remove censures of suspension or deposition only with the original presbytery's permission or after satisfying BCO 37-7 procedures; the original presbytery retains recourse under BCO 40-3 through 40-5.

title: Constitutional meaning of "Papists" in WCF 24.3
sub: The CCB declined to define "papists" abstractly, leaving the doctrinal question to presbyteries, judicial process, or an in thesi statement.

title: CRPR authority to review presbytery granting of ordination exceptions
sub: The CCB advised that a presbytery's granting of exceptions under BCO 21-4 is reviewable by the Committee on Review of Presbytery Records, which must report apparent violations accordingly.

title: RAO amendment omitting presbytery responses on ministers' stated differences
sub: The CCB found the proposed RAO amendment in conflict with BCO 21-4 because its reporting requirements failed to cover all possible presbytery responses to candidates' stated differences with the Standards.

title: Limiting TE voting rights in presbytery and General Assembly to pastors
sub: The CCB again found the overture in conflict with BCO 3-1 and 14-2, reaffirming that restricting voting to pastors disenfranchises non-pastor Teaching Elders.

title: Confessional subscription and amendment of exception-stating requirements
sub: An overture from Grace Presbytery amending BCO Preface II, chapter 16, and BCO 21-4 regarding doctrinal subscription was found in conflict with the second ordination vow.

title: Teaching Elders serving as corporate officers or trustees of a particular church
sub: The CCB advised that it is unconstitutional for Teaching Elders to serve as officers or trustees of the corporation of a particular church under BCO 4.

title: Proposed annotation to WCF defining term "Papist"
sub: The CCB ruled an overture to annotate the WCF to define "Papist" out of order, as no constitutional process or precedent exists for WCF annotations.

title: Relationship of assistant pastor to congregation under BCO 22
sub: An overture detailing how assistant pastors are called was found in conflict with BCO 3-2 and 12-2 through 12-4, as the relationship of an assistant pastor to a congregation is best left to the session.

title: Mandate requiring teaching elders to explain approved exceptions
sub: An overture requiring teaching elders to explain all approved exceptions was found in conflict with BCO Preliminary Principle 1 and WCF 20, as it could compel a man to teach what he does not believe.

title: Presbytery membership of a church outside its geographic bounds
sub: The CCB advised that a presbytery does not have constitutional authority under BCO 13-1 to include as a member a church located outside its bounds.

title: Committing all disciplinary matters to the Standing Judicial Commission
sub: A proposed amendment to BCO 15-4 committing all matters governed by the Rules of Discipline to the SJC was found not in conflict with the constitution.

title: Overture requiring all BCO changes referred to CCB and Bills and Overtures
sub: The CCB found this overture in conflict with RAO 13-1 and BCO 14-1, which permit BCO amendments of a particular nature to be referred to other committees and allow proposals to arise from other Assembly reports.

title: Mandating explanation of approved doctrinal exceptions by teaching elders
sub: A second overture on the same topic was found in conflict because it would compel a man to explain—or appear not to hold—a view his presbytery has already judged non-vital and approved.

title: Use of translations and translators in presbytery ordination examinations
sub: Whether translations and translators may be used in presbytery examinations is left to the presbytery's judgment as to whether qualifications are fully satisfied.

title: Language in which session records may be written and submitted
sub: CCB advised that a presbytery may permit session records kept or translated into English when submitted, provided they meet BCO standards.

title: Session authority to approve ministerial severance packages
sub: CCB advised that sessions may approve severance packages for ministers without congregational approval, as these are budgetary matters under BCO 12-5b.

title: Whether teaching that reprobate are in covenant with God is constitutional
sub: The CCB declined to answer a doctrinal question from Westminster Presbytery, stating that hypothetical issues of doctrine must be settled by adjudication.
disposition: advice given — hypothetical issues must be settled by adjudication

title: Effect of eliminating 'memorials' terminology on pending SJC cases
sub: The CCB advised that the Louisiana Presbytery case, commenced under the old BCO 40-5 as a memorial, must continue to be processed under the old BCO 40-5 and SJC Manual 16 provisions.

title: Overture mandating sprinkling as sole permissible baptismal mode
sub: A Presbytery of New Jersey overture to alter BCO 56-4.d was in conflict with WCF 28.3, which permits multiple modes of baptism, as the proposed language would allow only sprinkling.

title: Presbytery authority to decline approval of a pastoral call
sub: The CCB majority found an overture revising BCO 21-1 on a presbytery's right to decline pastoral calls not in conflict with the Constitution, while a minority concluded it was in conflict.
disposition: not in conflict (majority); minority reported in conflict

title: Restricting presbytery voting rights to financially contributing churches
sub: The CCB found that a proposed overture conditioning presbytery voting on church financial contributions was not in conflict with the constitution, though a minority dissented.

title: Presbytery discretion restricted by mandatory teaching of all exceptions
sub: An overture requiring a presbytery to allow a candidate to teach all his exceptions before approving him was found in conflict, as it violated presbytery's right to set ordination qualifications.

title: Presbytery standing rule banning persons removed from a PCA church
sub: The CCB opined that a proposed presbytery standing rule extending a church property ban to presbytery events was not in conflict with the PCA constitution.

title: CCB declining review of presbytery document on acceptable exceptions
sub: The CCB declined a non-judicial reference asking it to review a presbytery's list of acceptable and unacceptable ordination exceptions, citing the complexity of issues better resolved through judicial processes.

title: Requiring Apostles' Creed affirmation for church membership
sub: A Southeast Alabama overture requiring Apostles' Creed affirmation for membership was in conflict because 'profession of faith' in the BCO denotes commitment to Christ, not subscription to a doctrinal statement.

title: Proposed membership-transfer vows misusing 'profession of faith'
sub: A Southeast Alabama overture adding transfer-of-membership procedures was in conflict because it equated 'profession of faith' with doctrinal subscription and contained internal procedural contradictions.

title: Suspending a Teaching Elder During a Presbytery Investigation
sub: The CCB found Missouri Presbytery's overture to amend investigative procedures for a teaching elder was not in constitutional conflict, though a minority report argued otherwise.

title: Erecting a commission for investigation before process is instituted
sub: The overture was in conflict with BCO 31-2 and 32-2, which make clear that a judicial case does not exist until process is actually instituted.

title: Ad interim committee to revise the RAO and its funding
sub: The CCB found the overture to form an ad interim committee to revise the RAO in conflict with RAO 9-2 on the issue of funding.

title: Licensing a layman not pursuing a call to elder office
sub: The CCB stated that a man not a ruling or teaching elder and not pursuing eldership may nonetheless be licensed to preach per the plain wording of BCO 19-1.

title: Higher court assumption of original jurisdiction before lower court concludes
sub: A proposed amendment allowing a higher court to intervene when the lower court has had reasonable time was found to conflict with BCO 11-3's restriction against impinging on lower court authority.

title: Mandatory prior notice requirement for congregational withdrawal from PCA
sub: A proposed notice requirement for withdrawal conflicted with BCO 25-11, which allows a church to withdraw at any time for reasons it deems sufficient.

title: Session's Responsibility to Examine Children Transferring for Membership
sub: The CCB advised that sessions should give appropriate deference to prior admission decisions of other PCA sessions unless compelling reasons exist to do otherwise.

title: Pastoral oversight and discipline when spouses are in different jurisdictions
sub: CCB advised that no constitutional obstacle prevents courts from different presbyteries cooperating in marital reconciliation, though the BCO makes no provision for joint commissions of separate courts.

title: Presbytery's investigative duty when a new body holds evangelical beliefs
sub: The CCB advised that a presbytery's BCO 2-2 inquiry ends once it concludes a newly formed entity holds a fairly traditional evangelical set of beliefs.

title: Congregational requirement of supermajority vote to elect church officers
sub: The PCA Constitution does not permit a congregation to require that candidates for church office receive more than a majority vote to be elected.

title: Presbytery discretion in granting exceptions to BCO stated differences
sub: The CCB advised that a presbytery may ask candidates to state differences with the BCO but is not required to do so, and the BCO provides no mechanism for formally recognizing such differences.

title: Constitutional conflict in overture prohibiting deaconesses
sub: CCB found an overture prohibiting deaconesses in conflict with the Constitution because 'deaconess' need not denote an ordained office equivalent to deacon.

title: Constitutional conflict in overture prohibiting diaconal assistants
sub: CCB found an overture prohibiting commissioned or installed assistants to deacons in conflict with the Constitution on the same grounds as the deaconess overture.

title: Appointing unordained men and women to diaconal ministry roles
sub: CCB found an overture appointing unordained persons to diaconal ministry in conflict with the Constitution, including because it implied an unordained office contrary to BCO 17-1.

title: Ruling elder's participation in SJC case involving his own presbytery
sub: The CCB noted an exception where a ruling elder was deemed qualified to participate in reviewing a case involving his own presbytery, contrary to OMSJC 6.2(d).

title: Temporary governance for churches lacking ruling elders
sub: The CCB found that a proposed overture on temporary governance for elder-less churches may have conflicted with the constitution if read as prescriptive under BCO Preliminary Principles 2 and 6 and BCO 12-1.

title: Confusing use of 'admonition' in non-judicial rebuke context
sub: The CCB cautioned that using 'admonish' and 'admonition' outside their technical judicial-censure meaning in BCO 30 could cause confusion when applied to actions against church officers.

title: Clarifying What Triggers a Required Investigative Inquiry Under BCO 31-2
sub: The CCB found Pacific Northwest Presbytery's overture clarifying investigation triggers not in conflict, though a dissent warned it could mandate inquiry even on slight grounds.

title: Restricting removal of excommunication to the original court
sub: An overture requiring only the original session to remove excommunication conflicted with BCO 37-7, which allows a court with current jurisdiction to act after a member relocates.

title: Sufficiency of faith profession for visible church membership
sub: CCB found proposed overtures conflicting with BCO because the only required profession for church membership is faith in Jesus Christ, with the session judging qualifications.

title: Multisite church polity and scope of session and presbytery oversight
sub: The CCB advised that BCO 4 neither prescribes nor proscribes multisite polity, and that presbytery retains review and control over all worship sites, including new ones.

title: Defining Supporting Reasons Required for a Complaint or Appeal
sub: The CCB found Suncoast Florida Presbytery's overture adding a definition of supporting reasons for complaints or appeals was not in conflict if BCO 32-18 is read as addressing cases in process.

title: Proposed definition of terms in BCO 42 narrowing appeal grounds
sub: An overture adding a definitions section to BCO 42 was possibly in conflict with BCO 42-3 if the new definitions excluded grounds of appeal already recognized there.

title: Proposed BCO 43 definitions improperly narrowing complaint scope
sub: A Suncoast Florida overture to define terms in BCO 43 was found in conflict because it would restrict complaints to judicial-case matters, contradicting BCO 43-1's broader 'any act or decision' standard.

title: Lord's Supper administered to communicants at a separate site
sub: The CCB declined to give additional advice to Ohio Valley Presbytery on administering the Lord's Supper at a remote location, directing the matter to other courts of the church.

title: General Assembly authority to review and vote on SJC judicial decisions
sub: The CCB found an overture allowing the Assembly to vote on SJC decisions was in conflict with the Constitution and also assigned tasks beyond the CCB's own purview under RAO 8.2.b.

title: Allowing CCB to take exception to SJC case decisions
sub: The CCB found the proposed overture may have been in conflict with the Constitution due to ambiguity in its use of the word 'records' and the phrase 'any judicial cases.'

title: Defining the scope of "labor" for teaching elders under BCO 13-2
sub: The CCB advised that "labor" for teaching elders means needful ministry for Gospel dissemination, with each presbytery determining whether such labor is allowable within its bounds.

title: Recording candidate's views on male-only eldership at ordination
sub: No constitutional procedure exists to record a candidate's views on the BCO's requirements, nor is a candidate required to list his differences with its provisions.

title: Inactive or non-locally-elected elders as General Assembly commissioners
sub: A rotating-session inactive elder may be elected GA commissioner, but a ruling elder never elected in that congregation may not serve as its commissioner.

title: Requiring church officers to testify in disciplinary proceedings
sub: An overture to compel church officer testimony was possibly in conflict with BCO 35-3 and 35-4 regarding the two-witness requirement and exclusion of witnesses during others' testimony.

title: Requiring ministers without call to report annually to presbytery
sub: An overture mandating annual presbytery reporting for officers without call was in conflict with the Constitution because the new BCO 34-10 language conflicted with BCO 42-2.

title: Dual presbytery approval required for an uncalled TE living outside bounds
sub: The CCB advised that a TE member living and laboring outside his presbytery's bounds must obtain approval from both his home presbytery and the host presbytery.

title: Dual presbytery approval required for an uncalled TE living outside bounds
sub: The CCB advised that a TE member living and laboring outside his presbytery's bounds must obtain approval from both his home presbytery and the host presbytery.

title: General Assembly power to give directions to SJC on judicial decisions
sub: The CCB found an overture allowing the Assembly to direct SJC judicial reasoning was in conflict with the Constitution, because a commission concludes business as the court itself under BCO 15.

title: Requirement to submit and review minutes of a mission church evangelist
sub: The CCB found the overture in conflict with BCO 5-3.a because an evangelist, unlike governing bodies, has no minutes to submit under the BCO.

title: Overture precluding complaints while judicial case is pending
sub: A Pacific Northwest overture to clarify timing of higher-court complaint review was in conflict because it would bar filing any complaint against a court while a judicial case is in process.

title: Propriety of Absentee Ballots in Congregational Meetings
sub: The CCB advised that absentee ballots are barred whenever the BCO requires the congregation to convene or a majority of those present to carry a vote.

title: Scope of presbytery stated clerk's duty to disseminate information
sub: CCB advised that a stated clerk may provide information consistent with presbytery records, especially when requested by a former TE in connection with ecclesiastical litigation.

title: Validity of a transferred minister's ordination credentials
sub: The CCB advised that the minister in question was properly ordained and was a member in good standing of the presbytery at the time of his transfer.

title: Granting BCO chapter 59 on marriage full constitutional status
sub: A Calvary Presbytery overture to elevate BCO 59 to full constitutional status was in conflict because doing so is equivalent to amending the BCO and requires the same multi-stage approval process under BCO 26-2.

title: Adding male-only ordination language as fundamental to system of doctrine
sub: The overture was in conflict because designating male-only ordination an 'essential component' of ecclesiology improperly added it to the 'fundamental to our system of doctrine' standard.

title: Sabbatical provisions for ruling elders and deacons under BCO 24-7
sub: The proposed overture was in conflict because its use of 'officer' was broader than the definition of ruling elders and deacons in BCO 7-2.

title: Directing clerk to refer overtures on committees and agencies to Overtures Committee
sub: The CCB found the proposed RAO revision created ambiguity and could produce conflicting recommendations from two committees with no RAO procedure to resolve such floor conflicts.

title: Limiting formation of ad interim committees to presbytery overtures only
sub: The CCB found the overture in conflict with RAO 9-4, as the phrase 'exclusively submitted' directly contradicted existing provisions permitting other means of forming ad interim committees.

title: Stated supply moderator's role in local church discipline proceedings
sub: A presbytery-appointed stated supply and session moderator may participate in church discipline but, not being a session member, may not vote.

title: Session authority over church budget versus congregational call terms
sub: The CCB answered yes: under BCO 12-5.b, the session's duty to approve the budget supersedes the congregation's authority to set pastoral call terms in any fiscal year.

title: Application of BCO 24-1 examination process to re-election of officers
sub: BCO 24-1 applies fully to men with no prior official relationship to the church; for men already serving, lower courts determine which provisions apply at re-election.

title: Granting BCO 59 on Marriage Full Constitutional Status
sub: The CCB found Overture 24 from Tennessee Valley Presbytery, which sought to grant BCO 59's marriage provisions full constitutional status, to be in conflict with the Constitution.

title: Proposed Revision of BCO 59 Marriage Language and Consent
sub: The CCB found Calvary Presbytery's overture to revise BCO 59 in conflict with the Constitution, as proposed changes to marriage publication and consent conflicted with BCO Preliminary Principle 7, BCO 11-2, BCO 29-1, and WCF 20.2.

title: Routing Ad Interim Committee Recommendations Through Overtures Committee
sub: The CCB found Calvary Presbytery's overture requiring ad interim committee recommendations to go through the Overtures Committee in conflict with BCO 14-1.15 and productive of procedural ambiguity on the GA floor.

title: Overture defining roles of unordained deaconesses and deacon assistants
sub: CCB found Grace Presbytery's overture on deaconesses and women officers in conflict with the Constitution, noting the session, not the congregation, is the appointing body requiring vows.

title: Thirty-Day Notice for Church Withdrawal and Congregational Competency
sub: The CCB found Western Canada Presbytery's overture requiring thirty days' notice to leave the PCA was not in conflict, but a minority argued it violated congregational competency and civil sufficiency clauses.

title: Thirty-Day Notice for Church Withdrawal and Congregational Competency
sub: The CCB found Western Canada Presbytery's overture requiring thirty days' notice to leave the PCA was not in conflict, but a minority argued it violated congregational competency and civil sufficiency clauses.

title: Definite Suspension as Conclusion of Judicial Process Under BCO 30
sub: The CCB found Philadelphia Presbytery's overture on definite suspension in conflict with BCO 30-1, which states that definite suspension concludes the judicial process.

title: Granting BCO 59 on Marriage Full Constitutional Status
sub: The CCB found Overture 24 from Tennessee Valley Presbytery, which sought to grant BCO 59's marriage provisions full constitutional status, to be in conflict with the Constitution.

title: Permitting additional ruling elder representation at General Assembly
sub: The CCB found an overture from Northwest Georgia Presbytery to revise BCO 14-2 permitting additional ruling elder representation at the General Assembly was not in conflict with the Constitution.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Releasing Covenant Theological Seminary from General Assembly oversight
sub: The CCB found an overture encouraging CTS toward greater growth and release from Assembly oversight created a conflict with the RAO and Corporate By-Laws.
disposition: creates conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Promoting godly prudence in corporate prayer and worship at General Assembly
sub: The CCB found an overture amending RAO 10-3 to encourage godly prudence in planning corporate prayer and worship at the General Assembly was not in conflict with the Constitution.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Procedures for dissolving various pastoral relations under BCO 23-1
sub: The CCB found an overture from Central Indiana Presbytery amending BCO 23-1 on procedures for dissolving pastoral relations was not in conflict with the Constitution.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Proposal to disallow memorials via RAO amendment
sub: An overture from Pacific Northwest Presbytery to amend RAO 11 and 13 to disallow memorials was found not in conflict and adopted by the CCB.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Ad interim committee on domestic abuse and sexual assault
sub: An overture to form an ad interim committee addressing domestic abuse, domestic oppression, and sexual assault was found in conflict with RAO 9-3 but adopted by the CCB.
disposition: in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Allowing non-ordained persons on GA committees and boards
sub: An Ohio Valley Presbytery overture to amend BCO 14-1 to permit non-ordained persons to serve on GA committees and boards was found in conflict and adopted by the CCB.
disposition: in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Amending filing methods and deadlines for judicial cases
sub: An overture amending BCO 42-4, 43-2, and 43-3 to update methods and deadlines for filing judicial cases was found not in conflict and adopted by the CCB.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Floor nominations procedure under RAO 8-4
sub: An overture from Northern New England Presbytery to amend RAO 8-4 regarding floor nominations was found not in conflict and adopted by the CCB.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Non-ordained persons on committees and boards, second version
sub: A Southern New England Presbytery version of the overture to allow non-ordained persons on GA committees and boards was found in conflict and adopted by the CCB.
disposition: in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Preventing contradictory overture actions by multiple commissioner committees
sub: An overture to amend RAO 11-5 to prevent multiple committees of commissioners from taking contradictory actions on overtures was found to create internal conflict and was adopted by the CCB.
disposition: creates internal conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Creating offices of Moderator-Elect and Vice-Moderator
sub: An overture from Georgia Foothills Presbytery to amend the RAO to provide for a Moderator-Elect and Vice-Moderator was found not in conflict and adopted by the CCB.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Permitting video testimony by witnesses in judicial process
sub: A Houston Metro Presbytery overture to amend BCO 32-8 to allow video testimony by witnesses in church judicial proceedings was found in conflict and adopted by the CCB.
disposition: in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Conflicting overture actions by commissioner committees, second filing
sub: A Houston Metro Presbytery overture to amend RAO 11-5 to prevent contradictory actions on overtures by multiple committees of commissioners was found to create internal conflict and adopted by the CCB.
disposition: creates internal conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Non-ordained persons on committees and boards, Rocky Mountain version
sub: A Rocky Mountain Presbytery overture to amend BCO 14-1 permitting non-ordained persons to serve on GA committees and boards was found in conflict and adopted by the CCB.
disposition: in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Non-ordained persons on committees and boards, third version
sub: A Nashville Presbytery overture (version 3) to amend BCO 14-1 to allow non-ordained persons on GA committees and boards was found in conflict and adopted by the CCB.
disposition: in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Local session discretion to permit women serving as deacons
sub: This overture proposed amending multiple BCO provisions to allow individual sessions to decide locally whether women may serve as deacons; the CCB found it not in conflict.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Non-ordained persons serving on GA committees and boards
sub: This overture to allow non-ordained persons to serve on GA committees and boards was found by the CCB to be in conflict with the BCO.
disposition: in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Adding a Trinitarian vow to ordination vows
sub: This overture proposing to add a vow regarding the Trinity to BCO 57-5 was found not in conflict with the Constitution.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Clarifying SJC filing requirements and permitting electronic filings
sub: Proposed amendments to BCO 42-4, 43-2, 43-3, and OMSJC to clarify filing requirements and allow electronic filings were found not in conflict with the Constitution.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Clarifying the meaning of 'credible report' in BCO discipline
sub: Proposed amendments to BCO 40-4 and 40-5 to clarify what constitutes a 'credible report' were found not in conflict with the Constitution.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: SJC member eligibility for concurrent GA agency board service
sub: The CCB advised that a member of the GA's Standing Judicial Commission is eligible to serve concurrently on the board of a General Assembly agency.

title: Consecutive service on different GA committees without sabbatical
sub: The CCB advised that a GA permanent committee member who completed a full term is not eligible for consecutive service on a different committee or agency board without a sabbatical year.
disposition: No (not eligible); Adopted by the CCB

title: Effect of presbytery division on advice and consent voting
sub: The CCB advised that a newly erected presbytery may give advice and consent on BCO amendments already voted on by its predecessor, but individual presbyters may not vote a second time after transfer.
disposition: Yes to (1), No to (2); Adopted by the CCB

title: Overtures Committee's exclusive authority over constitutional amendments
sub: The CCB advised that the Overtures Committee has exclusive property over overtures requesting amendment to the Constitution under RAO 11-5.
disposition: Yes (exclusive property); Adopted by the CCB

title: Amending BCO 8-7 regarding chaplain ordination requirements
sub: An overture from Potomac Presbytery to amend BCO 8-7 regarding chaplains was found not in conflict with the Constitution and adopted.

title: Election procedures for pastor, associate pastor, and officers
sub: Proposed amendments to BCO 20-4, 24-3, and 24-4 regarding election of pastor, associate pastor, and officers were found not in conflict with the Constitution.

title: Session discretion on timing of officer candidate examinations
sub: An overture to amend BCO 24-1 allowing sessions discretion over the timing of portions of elder and deacon nominee examinations was found constitutionally vague.

title: Prohibiting ordination of men who self-identify as gay or homosexual Christians
sub: An overture to amend BCO 17 barring ordination of self-identified gay or homosexual Christians was withdrawn by Lowcountry Presbytery; no CCB advice was rendered.
disposition: withdrawn by submitting presbytery; no advice rendered

title: Number of members required to file an Overtures Committee minority report
sub: An overture amending RAO 15-6.s to revise the member threshold for filing Overtures Committee minority reports was found not in conflict and adopted.

title: Content requirements for Overtures Committee minority reports
sub: An overture amending RAO 15-6.s to prescribe the required content of minority reports was found not in conflict and adopted.

title: Requiring investigation to precede formal disciplinary process
sub: An overture amending BCO 31-2 and 32-2 to clarify that investigation must precede judicial process was found not in conflict and adopted.

title: Prohibiting electronic communications regarding General Assembly voting
sub: An overture to revise RAO 11-2 disallowing electronic communications about GA voting was found not in conflict, though not germane to the proposed RAO chapter, and adopted.
disposition: not in conflict but not germane to the RAO chapter proposed; Adopted

title: Disqualifying same-sex attracted men from ordination under BCO 7
sub: An overture from Westminster Presbytery to amend BCO 7 and related provisions disqualifying same-sex attracted men from ordination was found in conflict and adopted.

title: Clarifying funding procedures for ad interim committees
sub: An overture amending RAO 9-3 to clarify how ad interim committees are funded was found not in conflict and adopted.

title: Session discretion over timing of officer nominee examinations
sub: An overture amending BCO 24-1 to clarify that sessions have discretion over the timing of portions of officer-nominee examinations was found not in conflict and adopted.

title: Appealing a censure imposed without formal judicial process
sub: An overture amending BCO 38-1 and 42-2 to allow appeal of a censure in cases conducted without formal process was found not in conflict and adopted.

title: Pre-trial non-disciplinary suspensions in judicial proceedings
sub: An overture amending BCO 31-10 and 33-4 to address non-disciplinary suspensions prior to trial was found not in conflict and adopted.

title: Vote threshold for maintaining a censure during appeal
sub: An overture amending BCO 42-6 to specify the vote required to maintain a censure while an appeal is pending was found not in conflict and adopted.

title: Time limitations on prosecuting offenses under BCO 32-20
sub: An overture amending BCO 32-20 to address time considerations governing the prosecution of offenses was found not in conflict and adopted.

title: Ordination ban for men self-identifying as gay Christians
sub: The CCB found this overture to amend BCO 17 prohibiting ordination of men using gay-identity labels to be in conflict with existing BCO provisions, though it was adopted.

title: Permitting church courts to meet by telecommunication
sub: The CCB found that amending BCO 10 to authorize telecommunication meetings would create a conflict with existing BCO and RAO provisions, though the overture was adopted.

title: Requiring public announcement of excommunication
sub: The CCB determined that amending BCO 36 to mandate public excommunication would create a conflict with current BCO provisions, though the overture was adopted.

title: Codifying sequence of steps in discipline investigations
sub: The CCB found proposed amendments to BCO 31-2 prescribing an investigation sequence to be in conflict at multiple points with existing BCO provisions, though the overture was adopted.
disposition: in conflict at multiple points; Adopted

title: Adding BCO appendix of investigation practice suggestions
sub: The CCB found an overture adding a BCO appendix of investigation guidelines to be in conflict with the BCO, though the overture was adopted.

title: Amending BCO to clarify moral requirements for church office
sub: The CCB found that proposed amendments to BCO 21 and 24 clarifying moral qualifications for church office were not in conflict with the BCO, and the overture was adopted.

title: Process for converting assistant pastor to associate pastor
sub: The CCB found that amending BCO 22 to clarify the process for an assistant pastor becoming an associate pastor was not in conflict with the BCO, and the overture was adopted.

title: Confession document requirements for cases without process
sub: The CCB found that amending BCO 38-1 regarding confession documentation in cases without process was not in conflict with the BCO, and the overture was adopted.

title: Confession timing requirements in cases without process
sub: The CCB found that amending BCO 38-1 regarding timing of confession in cases without process was not in conflict with the BCO, and the overture was adopted.

title: Right to counsel in discipline cases without process
sub: The CCB found that amending BCO 38-1 regarding counsel for accused persons in cases without process was not in conflict with the BCO, and the overture was adopted.

title: Clarifying moral qualifications for ordained office holders
sub: The CCB found that Eastern Pennsylvania Presbytery's overture amending BCO 21-4 and 24-1 to clarify moral requirements for church office was not in conflict with the BCO, and was adopted.

title: Victim protection provisions in church discipline proceedings
sub: The CCB found that amending BCO 32-13 and 35-5 to allow victim protection measures in discipline proceedings was not in conflict with the BCO, and the overture was adopted.

title: Expanding witness eligibility under BCO 35-1
sub: CCB found no constitutional conflict in an overture to broaden the pool of eligible witnesses in disciplinary proceedings under BCO 35-1.

title: Procedure for BCO 40-5 reports alleging sessional delinquency
sub: CCB issued three opinions addressing procedure, anonymity, and standing when a presbytery receives a BCO 40-5 report alleging delinquency or grossly unconstitutional acts by a Session.
disposition: Answered (three opinions on procedure, anonymity, and standing); Adopted

title: Assistant Pastor standing to file complaints against Sessions
sub: CCB found an overture allowing assistant pastors to file complaints against Sessions under BCO 22-3 to be in conflict with the BCO.
disposition: in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Suspensions during pending process or delay under BCO 31-10
sub: CCB found no constitutional conflict in proposed amendments to BCO 31-10 and 33-4 governing suspensions during judicial process or delay.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Suspending judgment during appeal under BCO 42-6
sub: CCB found no constitutional conflict in an overture to amend BCO 42-6 regarding the suspension of judgment pending an appeal.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Percentage threshold for original jurisdiction requests under BCO 33-1
sub: CCB found in conflict an overture from Houston Metro Presbytery establishing a numerical percentage threshold for requesting original jurisdiction under BCO 33-1 and 34-1.
disposition: in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Numerical threshold for original jurisdiction requests under BCO 34-1
sub: CCB found in conflict a Calvary Presbytery overture setting a percentage threshold for original jurisdiction requests, touching BCO 34-1 and 11-4.
disposition: in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Percentage threshold for calling large-church congregational meetings
sub: CCB found no constitutional conflict in amending BCO 25-2 to add a new percentage threshold for calling congregational meetings in large churches.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Adding human-sexuality language from ad interim committee to BCO 16
sub: CCB found no constitutional conflict in an overture incorporating the Ad Interim Committee on Human Sexuality's language as a new paragraph BCO 16-4.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Restructuring SJC composition by amending BCO 15-4 and RAO 17-1
sub: CCB found no constitutional conflict in a Westminster Presbytery overture to change the membership composition of the Standing Judicial Commission.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Disqualifying from office men who identify as homosexual
sub: CCB found no constitutional conflict in an overture to amend BCO 7 disqualifying from ordained office men who identify as homosexual.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Requiring annual congregational meeting and financial reporting standards
sub: CCB found in conflict an overture to amend BCO 25-2 mandating annual congregational meetings and specific financial reporting standards.
disposition: in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Requiring open proceedings for ecclesiastical trials
