sub: An overture proposing to amend BCO 32-3 to mandate open proceedings for trials was found not in conflict with the BCO.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Stated Clerk's administrative role in processing allegations
sub: A proposed RAO 3-2 amendment adding Stated Clerk responsibilities for processing allegations was found not in conflict with the BCO.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Membership and quorum requirements for presbytery commissions
sub: A proposed amendment to BCO 15-2 clarifying presbytery commission membership and quorum was found not in conflict with the BCO.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Adding qualifications for ordination to BCO 16
sub: An overture from Northwest Georgia Presbytery proposing a new BCO 16-4 on ordination qualifications was found not in conflict with the BCO.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Timing requirements for filing a complaint under BCO 43
sub: A proposed amendment to BCO 43-2 and 43-3 regarding the timing of filing complaints was found ambiguous and adopted by the CCB.

title: Digital accessibility of statistical data under RAO 3-2
sub: An overture to amend RAO 3-2.h making statistical data digitally accessible was found not in conflict with the BCO.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Qualifications for ordination added to BCO 16 by Southeast Alabama
sub: Southeast Alabama Presbytery's overture to add BCO 16-4 on ordination qualifications was found not in conflict with the BCO.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Eligibility to submit overtures under RAO 11
sub: A proposed amendment to RAO 11-2 and 11-10 clarifying who may submit an overture was found not in conflict with the BCO.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Clarifying the role of a presbytery commission under BCO 15
sub: Houston Metro Presbytery's overture to amend BCO 15-1 and 15-3 clarifying a presbytery commission's role was found not in conflict with the BCO.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Potomac Presbytery's proposal on presbytery commission role
sub: Potomac Presbytery's overture to amend BCO 15-1 and 15-3 clarifying a presbytery commission's role was found not in conflict with the BCO.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Chaplain endorsement requirements added to BCO 8-7
sub: Pittsburgh Presbytery's overture adding chaplain endorsement requirements to BCO 8-7 was found in conflict with the BCO and adopted by the CCB.
disposition: in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Qualifications for church office added to BCO 16
sub: Pittsburgh Presbytery's overture proposing a new BCO 16-4 on qualifications for church office was found not in conflict with the BCO.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Congregational authority to set minimum voting age
sub: CCB found an overture to allow congregations to restrict voting by age internally inconsistent, vague, and in conflict with existing BCO provisions.
disposition: internally inconsistent and vague, in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Proposed additional ordination requirements in BCO 21-4 and 24-1
sub: CCB advised that Pittsburgh Presbytery's overture adding ordination requirement paragraphs to BCO 21-4 and 24-1 is not in conflict with the Constitution.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Authorizing a virtual meeting protocol in the RAO
sub: CCB found the Administrative Committee's proposed RAO 10-6 amendment establishing a virtual meeting protocol to be in conflict with the Constitution.
disposition: in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Proposed RAO 8-4 amendment and its conformity to constitutional standards
sub: CCB advised that Tennessee Valley Presbytery's proposed amendment to RAO 8.4 is in order and not in conflict with BCO or RAO standards.
disposition: in order and not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Seven overtures held over from the 48th to 49th General Assembly
sub: CCB advised that all seven overtures carried over from the 48th GA, touching discipline, appeals, and related BCO provisions, are not in conflict with the Constitution.
disposition: not in conflict (all seven); Adopted by the CCB

title: RPR's proposed amendment to RAO 16-4c and constitutional conformity
sub: CCB advised that the Review of Presbytery Records Committee's proposed change to RAO 16-4c is not in conflict with the Constitution.
disposition: not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB

title: Whether Committees of Commissioners may nominate substitute program coordinators
sub: CCB ruled that a Committee of Commissioners may only approve or disapprove nominees for program coordinator, and has no authority to nominate substitutes.
disposition: CoC may only approve or disapprove, not nominate substitute; Adopted by the CCB

title: Floor nomination procedure for the office of Stated Clerk
sub: CCB advised that floor nominations for Stated Clerk follow the same procedure as other contested offices, and nominees must be examined by the Theological Examining Committee.
disposition: floor nominations follow identical procedure to other contested offices; nominees must be examined by TEC; Adopted by the CCB

title: Proposed limits on General Assembly debate time under RAO
sub: CCB unanimously found James River Presbytery's overture to limit GA debate via RAO 14-9.h and 15-8.g not in conflict with the Constitution.

title: Chaplain authority to administer sacraments under BCO 8-6
sub: CCB unanimously found Tidewater Presbytery's overture to change BCO 8-6 regarding chaplain administration of sacraments to be in conflict with the Constitution.

title: Requiring criminal background checks for minister and officer candidates
sub: CCB unanimously found South Texas Presbytery's overture mandating criminal background checks for all minister and officer candidates not in conflict with the Constitution.

title: Committees of Commissioners reviewing committee and board compliance
sub: CCB unanimously found Southern New England Presbytery's overture expanding RAO 4-21.d to allow Committees of Commissioners to review compliance policies not in conflict with the Constitution.

title: Non-censure suspension of officers during investigation
sub: The CCB found that a proposed overture to add a non-censure suspension option during investigations (BCO 31-10) conflicts with the BCO as it currently stands.

title: Codifying biblical sexuality standard for church officers in BCO 7
sub: The CCB found that amending BCO 7 to codify the biblical standard for church officers related to human sexuality is not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Use of professional legal counsel in cases of process
sub: The CCB found that a proposed amendment to BCO 32-19 clarifying use of professional counsel in disciplinary proceedings conflicts with the existing BCO.

title: GA commissioners' right to object to SJC decisions
sub: The CCB found that amending BCO 15-4, 45-1, and 45-4 to allow General Assembly commissioners to register objections to SJC decisions is not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Allowing all persons as witnesses in disciplinary cases
sub: The CCB found that amending BCO 35-1 and 35-7 to permit all persons (without restriction) to serve as witnesses in cases of process is not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Professional counsel in cases of process — scope of participation
sub: The CCB found that a Northern California Presbytery overture amending BCO 32-19 regarding professional counsel in cases of process conflicts with the existing BCO.

title: Prohibiting women's exhortation, preaching, or teaching in worship
sub: The CCB found that amending BCO 53 to expressly disallow women from exhorting, preaching, or teaching in corporate worship is not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Officers' self-identification by biblical sins barred in BCO 7
sub: The CCB found that amending BCO 7 to prohibit church officers from self-describing by biblical sins (e.g., 'gay Christian') is not in conflict with the BCO.

title: BCO 7 qualifications limited to biological men who hold office
sub: The CCB found that an overture amending BCO 7 regarding the requirement that church officers be men is not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Specifying causes and processes for original jurisdiction requests
sub: The CCB found that amending BCO 33-1 and 34-1 to specify the causes and processes for requesting original jurisdiction is not in conflict with the BCO.

title: When minority reports are permitted under the RAO
sub: The CCB found that amending RAO 8-4.h, 17-1, and 19-2 to specify when minority reports are permitted in Assembly proceedings is not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Officers required to conform to biblical chastity in self-description
sub: The CCB found that amending BCO 8-2 and 9-3 to require officers to conform to biblical standards of chastity and sexual purity in self-description is not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Elder responsibilities and human sexuality language in BCO 8-3
sub: The CCB found no constitutional conflict in amending BCO 8-3 to incorporate AIC Report language on human sexuality for elder responsibilities.

title: Expedited, conflict-free investigations for cases with victims or moral failure
sub: The CCB found an overture to require expedited, conflict-free judicial investigations in BCO 31 to be in constitutional conflict, yet adopted it 8-0-0.

title: Restricting honorary titles for unordained persons under BCO 7-3
sub: The CCB found no constitutional conflict in amending BCO 7-3 to regulate the titling of unordained individuals.

title: Confessions and rights of offended parties in BCO 38-1
sub: The CCB found no constitutional conflict in amending BCO 38-1 to address confessions in relation to offended parties.

title: Whether one presbytery may involuntarily dissociate another presbytery from the PCA
sub: The CCB concluded that no procedure exists in the BCO or RAO by which one presbytery may involuntarily dissociate another from the PCA.

title: Recording examinees' stated differences in their own words under RAO 16-3.e.5
sub: The CCB found a proposed CRPR amendment requiring examinees' differences be recorded in their own words to be not in constitutional conflict.

title: Recording ministerial call arrangements in presbytery records under RAO 16-3.e.6
sub: The CCB found a proposed CRPR amendment on recording ministerial call arrangements in presbytery records to be not in constitutional conflict.

title: CRPR review scope for presbyteries with pending judicial cases
sub: The CCB found a proposed CRPR amendment addressing judicial cases and CRPR review under RAO 16-3.e.8 to be not in constitutional conflict.

title: Presbyteries' required responses to CRPR exceptions under RAO 16-10.a
sub: The CCB found a proposed CRPR amendment governing presbyteries' responses to Committee on Review of Presbytery Records exceptions to be not in constitutional conflict.

title: Witness eligibility standards in BCO 35-1 and 35-8
sub: The CCB found an overture to amend witness eligibility provisions in BCO 35-1 and 35-8 to be constitutionally vague.

title: Clarity in transfers of ordination between presbyteries under BCO 13-6
sub: The CCB found no constitutional conflict in amending BCO 13-6 to provide greater clarity in the process for transferring ordination between presbyteries.

title: Granting constitutional status to BCO 53 on preaching
sub: The CCB found no constitutional conflict in an overture to grant constitutional status to BCO 53 governing the ministry of preaching.

title: Study committee to review and reform judicial rules
sub: CCB found this overture in conflict (with existing provisions), yet adopted 8-0-0, proposing a study committee to examine changes to PCA judicial rules under RAO 9-3.

title: Mandatory background checks as condition for church office
sub: Overture to amend BCO 13-6 requiring background checks for church officers was found in conflict with existing BCO provisions but adopted 6-1-1.

title: Clarifying the amendment process for RAO provisions
sub: Overture to amend RAO 11-5 to clarify how RAO amendments are made was found not in conflict and adopted 6-1-1.

title: Adding Great Commission language to officer qualifications
sub: Overture to add a Great Commission requirement to BCO 12-5 was found not in conflict with the BCO and adopted unanimously 8-0-0.

title: Presbytery care obligations for teaching elders under BCO 13-9
sub: Overture to amend BCO 13-9 to add presbytery care requirements for teaching elders was found not in conflict and adopted 8-0-0.

title: Enrollment data reporting requirement for PCA higher education institutions
sub: Overture to amend RAO 4-21.d requiring enrollment data from PCA-affiliated higher education institutions was found not in conflict and adopted 8-0-0.

title: Restricting ordination to biological males under BCO 7-2
sub: Overture to amend BCO 7-2 explicitly limiting ordination to biological males was found not in conflict with the BCO and adopted unanimously 8-0-0.

title: Background checks required for ordination and church office
sub: Overture to amend BCO 13-6, 21-4, and 24-1 to mandate background checks for church officers was found not in conflict and adopted 8-0-0.

title: Parallel overture on background checks for church office holders
sub: A second overture (from Ohio Presbytery) to amend BCO 13-6, 21-4, and 24-1 requiring background checks was also found not in conflict and adopted 8-0-0.

title: Witness eligibility standards in church judicial proceedings
sub: Overture to amend BCO 35-1 and 35-8 regarding who may serve as a witness in judicial cases was found not in conflict and adopted 5-1-2.

title: Allowing venue change in church judicial proceedings
sub: Overture to amend BCO 41 permitting venue changes in judicial cases was found in conflict with existing BCO provisions but nonetheless adopted 8-0-0.

title: Systematic overhaul of BCO judicial process chapters 31, 32, and 35
sub: Overture proposing broad changes to BCO 31, 32, and 35 governing judicial process was found in grave conflict with multiple BCO provisions but adopted 8-0-0.

title: Prohibition against interlocutory appeal by complaint under BCO 43
sub: The CCB found the proposed overture to change BCO 43-1's prohibition against interlocutory appeals by complaint to be internally inconsistent and in conflict with existing BCO provisions.
disposition: internally inconsistent and in conflict; Adopted 8-0-0

title: Clarifying teaching elder presbytery membership in BCO 13-2
sub: The CCB found the proposed amendment to BCO 13-2 clarifying teaching elder presbytery membership not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Mandatory background checks for church office under BCO 13-6
sub: The CCB found a proposed overture requiring background checks for church office to be in conflict with the BCO and WCF Preliminary Principles.

title: Background checks for officers under BCO 13-6, 21-4, and 24-1
sub: The CCB found a South Texas Presbytery overture requiring background checks for church office not in conflict with BCO 13-6, 21-4, and 24-1.

title: Expanding who may assist in a BCO 31-2 investigation
sub: The CCB found a proposed amendment expanding who may assist in a judicial investigation under BCO 31-2 not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Expanding accused persons' representation before church courts
sub: The CCB found the proposed amendment to BCO 32-19 broadening representation of accused persons before church courts not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Examining transferring ministers' character and family management
sub: The CCB found the proposed amendment to BCO 13-6 adding personal character and family management to examinations of transferring ministers not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Resolving conflict between RAO 16-6 and BCO 40-5
sub: The CCB found the proposed amendment to RAO 16-6.c.1 to eliminate its conflict with BCO 40-5 not itself in conflict with the BCO.

title: BCO 53 amendment restricting preaching exclusively to men
sub: The CCB found the proposed addition to BCO 53 to ensure only men preach in conflict with existing BCO provisions, adopted by a divided vote.

title: Presbytery exit interview prior to dissolution of a minister's call
sub: The CCB found the proposed amendment to BCO 23-1 requiring a presbytery exit interview before dissolution of a call not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Changes in permanent committee and agency policy under BCO 14-1
sub: The CCB found the proposed amendment to BCO 14-1 regarding changes in permanent committee and agency policy not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Dissolution of call for ministers employed by a committee or agency
sub: The CCB found the proposed amendment to BCO 23 addressing dissolution of call for committee- or agency-employed ministers in conflict with BCO 11-4 and BCO 23.

title: Proposed amendment to RAO 16-3.e on presbytery records review
sub: The CCB found the CRPR's proposed amendment to RAO 16-3.e not in conflict with the Constitution, adopting the finding 8-0-0.

title: Proposed amendment to RAO 16-6.c.1 on presbytery records review
sub: The CCB found the CRPR's proposed amendment to RAO 16-6.c.1 not in conflict with the Constitution, adopting the finding 8-0-0.

title: Court's authority to investigate before filing charges under BCO 32-2
sub: The CCB found the proposed amendment to BCO 32-2 clarifying a court's investigative authority both in conflict with and ambiguous in relation to the BCO.

title: Full constitutional status for BCO chapters 60–63 on church property
sub: The CCB found the overture granting BCO 60–63 full constitutional status not in conflict with the Constitution, adopted 7-1-0.

title: Flexibility for candidates applying to come under presbytery care
sub: The CCB found the proposed amendment to BCO 18-2 allowing flexible application procedures for candidates not in conflict with the Constitution.

title: Advisors and original jurisdiction in process against a teaching elder
sub: The CCB found the proposed amendment to BCO 34-1 regarding advisors and original jurisdiction in TE disciplinary process not in conflict with the Constitution.

title: Requiring ruling elders and deacons to state confessional differences at ordination
sub: The CCB found the proposed amendment to BCO 24-1 requiring REs and deacons to disclose confessional differences not in conflict with the Constitution.

title: Assembly committee and agency members as non-voting advisors under BCO 14-1
sub: The CCB found the proposed amendment making AC members from committees and agencies non-voting advisory members not in conflict with the Constitution.

title: Congregational consent requirement for calling assistant pastors under BCO 22
sub: The CCB found the proposed amendment affirming congregational consent for assistant pastors ambiguous, as it did not address how current assistant pastors would be treated upon adoption.
disposition: ambiguous (does not address treatment of current Assistant Pastors upon adoption); Adopted 7-0-1

title: Restricting distribution of the Lord's Supper to ordained officers
sub: The CCB found the proposed amendment to BCO 58-5 limiting Lord's Supper distribution to officers not in conflict with the Constitution, adopted 8-0-0.

title: Limiting congregational voting to members in good standing under BCO 25-1
sub: The CCB found the proposed amendment consistent with current BCO language but noted it did not resolve a possible conflict with BCO 25-7 and 25-11.
disposition: consistent with current BCO language but does not address possible conflict with BCO 25-7 and 25-11; Adopted 8-0-0

title: Court's permissible interactions with an offended party under BCO 38-1
sub: The CCB found the proposed amendment to BCO 38-1 specifying ways a court may interact with an offended party not in conflict with the Constitution.

title: Definite suspension from office language added to BCO 36-4
sub: Proposed amendment to BCO 36-4 adding definite suspension from office language was found not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Conforming BCO 36-5 suspension language to BCO 37-3
sub: Proposed amendment aligning BCO 36-5's suspension-from-office language with BCO 37-3 was found not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Revision of membership vows and their conformity to Westminster Standards
sub: Proposed revision of BCO 57-5 membership vows was found not in conflict with the BCO, though the CCB urged careful attention to Westminster Standards.
disposition: not in conflict with BCO; CCB advises careful attention to theological questions raised in light of the Westminster Standards; Adopted 8-0-0

title: Minimum voting age and meaning of 'regular standing' in BCO
sub: Proposed amendment clarifying 'regular standing' and minimum voting age in BCO 20-3, 24-3, and 25-1 was found in conflict with the BCO.

title: Supplemental judges permitted for session-level disciplinary trials
sub: Proposed amendment to BCO 41-3 allowing supplemental judges for a session trial was found not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Session's role in examining officer nominees under BCO 24-1
sub: Proposed amendment to BCO 24-1 clarifying a session's role in examining officer nominees was found not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Associate membership class for honorably retired teaching elders
sub: Proposed amendment to BCO 46-4 creating associate membership for honorably retired TEs was found in conflict with the BCO.

title: GA handling of an SJC minority decision under BCO 15-5
sub: Proposed amendment to BCO 15-5 clarifying how the General Assembly handles an SJC minority decision was found not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Calling and dissolution of TE relationships for needful work
sub: Proposed amendments to BCO 8-4, 20-1, 21-1, 21-12, and 23-1 regarding TE relationships for needful work were found in conflict with and ambiguous under the BCO.

title: Whole-court vote required to suspend officer's functions during process
sub: Proposed amendment requiring the entire court to vote before suspending an officer's functions during BCO 31-10 process was found not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Elevating a censure without a new trial under BCO 36
sub: Proposed amendment to BCO 36 providing for censure elevation without a wholly new trial was found to be ambiguous under the BCO.

title: Examination of young persons for admission to the sealing ordinances
sub: Proposed amendment to BCO 57-2 regarding examination of young persons for admission to the sealing ordinances was found not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Requiring session and diaconate membership reporting in RAO 16
sub: The CCB found an overture amending RAO 16 to mandate statistical reporting on session and diaconate membership and duties to be in conflict with existing BCO provisions.

title: Session member serving as moderator in judicial cases
sub: The CCB found an overture allowing a session to elect one of its own members as moderator in judicial cases to be not in conflict with the BCO.

title: New case required to elevate indefinite suspension to excommunication
sub: The CCB found an overture requiring a new judicial case and process before indefinite suspension may be elevated to excommunication to be in conflict with the BCO.

title: Process for elevating suspension from office to deposition
sub: The CCB found an overture clarifying the procedural steps for elevating suspension from office to deposition to be not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Recording all parts of a church trial under BCO 35-9
sub: The CCB found an overture requiring that all parts of a trial be recorded under BCO 35-9 to be not in conflict with the BCO.

title: Scope of Administrative Committee statistical reporting under RAO 4-11
sub: The CCB found an overture specifying the scope of statistical reporting by the Administrative Committee to be not in conflict with the BCO or RAO.

title: Whether all RAO amendments require CCB review before GA vote
sub: The CCB ruled that all RAO amendments require CCB review per RAO 8-2.b.3, but no provision requires Overtures Committee approval for RAO amendments originating from CRPR or the SJC.
disposition: All RAO amendments require CCB review per RAO 8-2.b.3; no provision requires Overtures Committee approval for RAO amendments from CRPR or SJC; Adopted 8-0-0

title: Timeliness of overtures received by April 25 deadline
sub: The CCB ruled that overtures received on or by April 25 are timely under RAO 11-6's 60-day deadline and must be officially handled at the current General Assembly.
disposition: Overtures received on or by April 25 are timely per RAO 11-6 (60-day deadline); Adopted 8-0-0

title: CCB authority to review SJC compliance and prior decisions
sub: The CCB declined a reference from Highlands Presbytery, ruling it lacks authority to render opinions on prior SJC decisions, which are final decisions of the General Assembly.
disposition: CCB declined to accede to the reference; SJC decisions are final decisions of the GA (BCO 15-5); CCB does not render opinions on previous SJC decisions; Adopted 8-0-0

title: Congregational meeting minutes as part of session records reviewed by presbytery
sub: The CCB ruled that congregational meeting minutes are part of session records subject to presbytery review, and sessions must submit them if the presbytery requires it.
disposition: CCB acceded to the reference; Congregational meeting minutes are part of Session records subject to Presbytery review; if Presbytery requires them, Sessions must submit them; Adopted 8-0-0

title: SJC proposed amendment to RAO 17-3
sub: The CCB found the SJC's proposed amendment to RAO 17-3 to be not in conflict with the BCO, and the Assembly adopted it unanimously.

title: AC Committee of Commissioners authority to nominate a Stated Clerk
sub: The CCB ruled that the Administrative Committee's Committee of Commissioners may not make a substitute nomination for Stated Clerk when the matter does not arise from AC recommendations.
disposition: No — AC CoC may not make a substitute nomination for Stated Clerk if the matter does not arise in AC recommendations; Adopted 8-0-0

title: Floor nominations for Stated Clerk when AC brings no nominee
sub: The CCB ruled that floor nominations are permitted under RAO 3-4 even if the Administrative Committee does not bring a Stated Clerk nominee.
disposition: Yes — floor nominations are allowed; RAO 3-4 second sentence provides GA right to select Stated Clerk regardless of AC nominee; Adopted 8-0-0

title: CRPR proposal to add new RAO 16-11 not in conflict
sub: The CCB found CRPR Recommendation 9, which adds a new section RAO 16-11, not in constitutional conflict with existing rules.

title: CRPR amendment adding sentences to RAO 16-3.b not in conflict
sub: The CCB found CRPR Recommendation 10, adding two sentences to RAO 16-3.b, not in constitutional conflict with existing rules.

title: CRPR amendment inserting new RAO 16-3.e subsection not in conflict
sub: The CCB found CRPR Recommendation 11, inserting a new subsection 6 into RAO 16-3.e and renumbering, not in constitutional conflict.
